claim_status: fresh-host run-record template only; no independent fresh-host reproduction, no owner-approved clean-VM equivalent, no command execution evidence, no retained bootstrap/doctor/check/xtask logs, no PB-009 readiness promotion, no broad implementation readiness, no preview readiness, no beta readiness, no stable readiness, no production readiness, and no Chrome-class claim

required_fields: run host is an independent fresh reference host or owner-approved clean VM equivalent
operating system edition, version, architecture, patch level, locale, timezone, shell, terminal, CPU, memory, disk, virtualization state, network posture, and privilege level are recorded

prior Turing build outputs, Cargo target directories, Rustup toolchain cache, package cache, downloaded installers, local profiles, and generated files are declared absent or covered by an owner-approved waiver
waiver records include owner, reason, scope, expiry, and cleanup evidence
evidence_artifacts: waiver record when a clean VM is used

required_fields: remote URL, branch, commit hash, checkout command, Git version, line-ending configuration, and ignored-output policy are recorded
source-tree status is captured before bootstrap, after doctor, after check, and after xtask commands
the record distinguishes source-controlled changes from generated build output and local caches
untracked installers, archives, credentials, local profiles, and generated junk are rejected
evidence_artifacts: git status before and after commands

required_fields: Rust, Cargo, Git, and host-tool observations
success_evidence: environment prerequisites are printed or linked
no installer, downloaded archive, or generated build output is committed
failure_evidence: environmental, source-controlled, network-dependent, or nondeterministic classification

success_evidence: no host configuration is silently changed
failure_evidence: missing prerequisite or host mismatch classification

success_evidence: CARGO_TARGET_DIR stayed outside durable source when unset
no source-tree junk remains after the run

required_fields: reason if wrapper execution is the owner-approved equivalent
success_evidence: xtask bootstrap path matches wrapper behavior or an owner-approved equivalence reason is recorded

required_fields: reason if wrapper execution is the owner-approved equivalent
success_evidence: xtask doctor --ci path matches wrapper behavior or an owner-approved equivalence reason is recorded

required_fields: reason if wrapper execution is the owner-approved equivalent
success_evidence: xtask check path matches wrapper behavior or an owner-approved equivalence reason is recorded

cache_and_artifact_controls: record CARGO_TARGET_DIR, Cargo cache root, Rustup toolchain cache, temp directory, and repository root before command execution
hash retained bootstrap, doctor, check, and xtask logs without committing generated build output
confirm build output, logs, temporary archives, and generated caches stay outside durable source or are explicitly ignored
record cleanup evidence for temporary directories and failed partial runs
source_tree_cleanliness: capture source-tree status before command execution
capture source-tree status after bootstrap, doctor, check, and xtask commands
classify every changed, untracked, ignored, or generated path
reject records that hide dirty-state output or omit generated-output cleanup
failure_denominator: record all attempted commands, not only successful commands
classify failures as environmental, source-controlled, network-dependent, nondeterministic, or owner-waived
preserve timeout, cancellation, interrupted-run, disk-full, permission, cache, and toolchain failures
keep failed runs in the denominator until an owner accepts a rerun policy
prohibited_evidence: same-host reruns are prohibited as independent fresh-host reproduction evidence
current-host diagnostics are prohibited from promoting PB-009
owner-approved clean-VM equivalence is prohibited without owner, reason, scope, expiry, and cleanup proof
records that omit exit codes, source-tree status, retained logs, cache roots, target directory, or failure classification are prohibited
committed installers, downloaded archives, credentials, local profiles, generated build output, or temporary junk are prohibited
